Table 2. Main results for page-level retrieval and reranking with first-stage retriever ColQwen (Faysse et al., 2025).
Figure 5. The reranking input prompt template and example target generation sequence for ZipRerank with Qwen3-VL.
Table 3. Ablation study of ZipRerank on DSEwiki−ss.

Figure 2. Overview of the ZipRerank framework. ZipRerank integrates a two-stage training pipeline: (i) listwise pretraining on large-scale text data rendered as images, followed by (ii) vision-centric finetuning with VLM-teacher-distilled soft supervision. The efficient inference design combines query-aware visual token pruning and single-token listwise scoring, enabling end-to-end reranking in a single LLM forward pass.
Table 7. Model checkpoints used in experiments.
Table 6. Training hyperparameters for Stage 1 and Stage 2.
Figure 4. Parameter study on the effect of the number of input passages k on reranking effectiveness (Recall@1, 3, 5) and latency (LLM Time in ms) on first stage results from DSEwiki−ss.

Table 5. Robustness to Stage-2 teacher strength. We compare teacher models with ZipRerank models trained using their generated rankings. Ma-Avg stands for Macro Average, and Mi-Avg stands for Micro Average of recall@k scores of the individual datasets.
Figure 3. Parameter study on the effect of image token keep ratio ρ on reranking effectiveness (Recall@1, 3, 5) and latency (LLM Time in ms) on first stage results from DSEwiki−ss.
